threat_description: An attacker could spoof the identity of an authorized developer or CI/CD pipeline to deploy malicious infrastructure changes.
mitigation: Enforce strong authentication (e.g., Azure AD service principals with MFA), restrict pipeline triggers, and sign all Terraform plans before apply.

threat_description: Authorized changes provisioned by Terraform cannot be reliably traced back to a unique actor, enabling repudiation of malicious or accidental changes.
mitigation: Enable detailed logging and audit trails in Azure Activity Logs, require code reviews, and attach immutable identifiers to pipeline runs.

threat_description: An attacker may spoof client identities (e.g., forge HTTP headers or tokens) to call front‐end or back‐end Function APIs.
mitigation: Implement strong authentication/authorization on APIs (e.g., Azure AD, API keys), validate tokens, and enforce origin checks.

threat_description: Clients may deny having sent certain requests (e.g., malicious or abusive API calls) due to lack of proper request logging.
mitigation: Log all incoming requests with timestamps, source IPs, and authentication context in a tamper‐evident store.

threat_description: An attacker spoofs Terraform provider endpoints or plugins to inject malicious code during apply.
mitigation: Validate plugin checksums, use signed provider binaries, restrict provider sources.

threat_description: An attacker tampers with local .tf files or state files to change resource definitions or inject secrets.
mitigation: Store state in secure remote backend with encryption and MFA-protected access; enable immutability.

threat_description: Changes applied by Terraform cannot be repudiated due to lack of authenticated commit metadata or signed runs.
mitigation: Require signed commits, enforce CI pipeline identity, and log Terraform runs with immutable audit.

threat_description: Sensitive variables (e.g., credentials) in Terraform plan or logs are exposed to unauthorized parties.
mitigation: Use secret management (Azure Key Vault), redact logs, and restrict access to plan outputs.

threat_description: An attacker floods the Terraform Engine with malformed or excessively large modules to exhaust local resources.
mitigation: Enforce size limits on modules, monitor resource usage, and isolate runs in containerized CI.

threat_description: Privileged Terraform process is tricked into running elevated local-exec scripts that yield root on the host.
mitigation: Run local-exec in sandboxed containers with least privileges and validate scripts before execution.

threat_description: An attacker spoofs management API calls to the App Service Plan to alter scaling or routing.
mitigation: Use Azure RBAC, enforce Azure AD tokens, and validate API caller identity.

threat_description: An attacker tampers with plan configuration (e.g., SKU, region) to run unauthorized or costly instances.
mitigation: Lock critical resources, enforce policy-as-code (Azure Policy), and review plan changes via PR.

threat_description: Scaling events or configuration changes are not logged or attributed, enabling repudiation.
mitigation: Enable diagnostic logs for App Service Plan and send to secure SIEM.

threat_description: Plan metrics or logs leak internal topology or usage data to unauthorized viewers.
mitigation: Restrict diagnostic log access, encrypt logs at rest, and audit access.

threat_description: An attacker sends excessive traffic to hosted apps, exhausting plan CPU or memory.
mitigation: Enable Autoscale rules, configure WAF/DDoS Protection, and set rate limits.

threat_description: Compromise of one Function App allows privilege escalation to manage the entire App Service Plan.
mitigation: Use separate plans for different trust levels, apply Just-In-Time access and RBAC.

threat_description: An attacker spoofs management requests to swap function app code or settings.
mitigation: Secure deployment endpoints with Azure AD and TLS client certs, validate callers.

threat_description: An attacker tampers function app configuration or code package in blob storage before startup.
mitigation: Use signed packages, enable storage account immutability, and scan packages.

threat_description: Unauthorized or unlogged changes to function app settings hamper traceability.
mitigation: Enable Activity Logs for function app settings and integrate with SIEM.

threat_description: Sensitive environment variables (e.g., AZ_DB_KEY) are exposed in logs or via debug endpoints.
mitigation: Mask sensitive app settings, disable verbose debugging, and scope credentials.

threat_description: Traffic spikes or malicious payloads cause the backend function to crash or throttle.
mitigation: Implement request throttling, quotas, input validation, and WAF policies.

threat_description: Compromised function code or config allows escape to host or access control elevation.
mitigation: Enable sandboxing, minimize host-level permissions, and apply code scanning.

threat_description: An attacker spoofs front-end deployment endpoint to push malicious UI code.
mitigation: Secure CI/CD pipeline with Azure AD, validate package signatures before deploy.

threat_description: UI code or configuration is tampered in transit or at rest before function startup.
mitigation: Use storage immutability, enforce HTTPS-only blob access, and scan packages.

threat_description: Front-end app settings changes are not audited, allowing untracked malicious updates.
mitigation: Enable App Service audit logs and integrate with log analytics.

threat_description: Client-side secrets or API URLs are exposed in console logs or error pages.
mitigation: Remove secrets from client bundle, use safe error handling, and enforce CSP.

threat_description: A flood of asset requests crashes the front-end host or increases latency.
mitigation: Use CDN with rate limiting, enable DDoS Protection, and cache assets.

threat_description: Compromised front-end allows injection of malicious scripts leading to privilege escalation in browser context.
mitigation: Implement strict CSP, sanitize inputs, and enable Subresource Integrity.

threat_description: An attacker spoofs SSH client identity to gain shell access.
mitigation: Use key-based SSH with strong passphrases, disable root login, and restrict IPs.

threat_description: An attacker tampers with VM disk or startup scripts to install backdoors.
mitigation: Enable Azure Disk Encryption, use signed extensions, and enforce image integrity.

threat_description: Malicious changes made via console or API are not logged, enabling repudiation.
mitigation: Enable Azure Activity Logs and guest OS audit logs with secure forwarding.

threat_description: Sensitive VM metadata or SSH sessions are exposed due to improper logging or debug tools.
mitigation: Disable metadata endpoint access from inside VM, secure serial console, and audit logs.

threat_description: Resource exhaustion (CPU, disk I/O) on VM causes service disruption.
mitigation: Monitor resource metrics, set quotas, and apply host-based intrusion protection.

threat_description: Compromise of application on VM leads to privilege escalation to host/system account.
mitigation: Use least-privilege service accounts, apply kernel hardening and regular patching.

threat_description: An attacker spoofs the CustomScriptExtension endpoint to deliver malicious scripts.
mitigation: Validate Azure extensions with signed packages; restrict extension updates via RBAC.

threat_description: An adversary tampers with scripts fetched from blob storage before execution on the VM.
mitigation: Use blob storage immutability policies, signed script packages, and checksums.

threat_description: Execution events of the extension are not logged or attributed, enabling repudiation of malicious actions.
mitigation: Enable diagnostics on VM extensions and forward logs to a central SIEM.

threat_description: Sensitive data in scripts (e.g., storage SAS) is exposed in logs or stdout.
mitigation: Avoid embedding secrets in scripts; use managed identities and redact logs.

threat_description: Malicious or malformed script payloads crash the extension and block provisioning.
mitigation: Validate script inputs, use timeouts, and isolate extension execution.

threat_description: Script runs with elevated privileges and installs persistent backdoors on the VM.
mitigation: Run extensions under least-privilege identity, disable unnecessary privileges, and audit changes.

threat_description: An attacker spoofs Automation Account API calls to modify runbook or credentials.
mitigation: Enforce Azure AD authentication, restrict access via RBAC, and enable OAuth tokens.

threat_description: Configuration of the Automation Account is tampered (e.g., identity assignments changed).
mitigation: Lock resource groups, apply Azure Policy for identity assignments, and enable activity logs.

threat_description: Changes to Automation Account (e.g., permission grants) are not auditable, enabling repudiation.
mitigation: Enable Azure Activity Logs, designate approved approvers, and require change requests.

threat_description: Secrets or credentials stored in Automation Account runbooks are exposed to unauthorized users.
mitigation: Use Azure Key Vault for secrets, enable runbook encryption, and restrict viewer roles.

threat_description: High load of runbook jobs saturates Automation Account, delaying critical tasks.
mitigation: Set maximum concurrent jobs, monitor queue length, and throttle runbook triggers.

threat_description: Compromised runbook or account identity elevates privileges to manage subscription resources.
mitigation: Grant minimal RBAC roles to Automation identity, use Just-In-Time access, and rotate credentials.

threat_description: An attacker spoofs runbook execution requests to run arbitrary scripts under the account identity.
mitigation: Require MFA on runbook start, verify caller identity, and use approval workflows.

threat_description: Runbook code is tampered (e.g., injection of malicious PowerShell commands).
mitigation: Use source control for runbook, enable change reviews, and sign scripts.

threat_description: Runbook executions cannot be repudiated due to missing execution logs or metadata.
mitigation: Enable runbook logging and audit trails in a write-once storage account.

threat_description: Sensitive parameters (e.g., client ID) passed to runbook are exposed in logs.
mitigation: Use secure variables in Automation, mask parameters in logs, and rotate IDs.

threat_description: Adversary floods runbook jobs to exhaust quotas and prevent legitimate automation.
mitigation: Implement job concurrency limits, alerts on runbook failures, and approval gating.

threat_description: Runbook executes code that escalates to higher privileged roles (owner) via Automation identity.
mitigation: Limit Automation identity RBAC scope, apply PIM for elevation, and review assignments.

threat_description: Adversary spoofs or replaces local create-table.py to populate malicious data in CosmosDB.
mitigation: Verify script integrity with checksums, restrict local file modifications, and use private repo.

threat_description: The data population script is tampered to leak credentials or inject malicious documents.
mitigation: Use signed scripts, run in isolated container, and scan code before execution.

threat_description: Population runs are not logged, allowing denial of execution or denial of malicious changes.
mitigation: Log every run with metadata, write to tamper-evident storage, and require approvals.

threat_description: Output of the script (e.g., endpoint, key) is exposed on local console or logs.
mitigation: Suppress secrets in logs; retrieve credentials securely via Key Vault.

threat_description: Large or malformed dataset insertion exhausts CosmosDB throughput, causing service lag.
mitigation: Rate-limit inserts, validate input size, and monitor RU consumption.

threat_description: Script runs with default user permissions escalate to database admin via connection string.
mitigation: Use least-privilege database user, rotate keys, and restrict script access.

threat_description: Attacker spoofs the environment variable replacement script to inject malicious settings.
mitigation: Validate script origin, use version-controlled templates, and restrict local write access.

threat_description: Tampering with local.settings.json patching introduces insecure or hardcoded credentials.
mitigation: Use secret references (Key Vault), enforce JSON schema validation, and audit changes.

threat_description: Changes to environment variable injection are not tracked, enabling repudiation of config errors.
mitigation: Version control patched files, log patch events, and require code review.

threat_description: Patched environment files expose connection strings or keys in local workspace logs.
mitigation: Redact secrets in logs, use secure environment injection, and limit workspace access.

threat_description: Large or malformed environment files cause provisioning to hang or fail.
mitigation: Validate file structure and size before patching, enforce timeouts.

threat_description: The replacement process runs with elevated shell privileges to write to protected directories.
mitigation: Run scripts under non‐privileged user, isolate in container, and limit filesystem access.

threat_description: An attacker spoofs or replaces the upload script to inject malicious asset URLs.
mitigation: Store scripts in version control, verify checksums before execution.

threat_description: Tampering with the patch process corrupts React HTML/JS, breaking application behavior.
mitigation: Validate file integrity with schema checks and signed templates.

threat_description: Upload actions are not logged, allowing repudiation of malicious or faulty uploads.
mitigation: Log all upload operations with timestamps and script hashes to an immutable store.

threat_description: Patch output (URLs, tokens) is printed to console, exposing internal endpoints.
mitigation: Redact sensitive information, use secure logging, and environment variables for endpoints.

threat_description: Malformed or large patch operations cause the script to hang or crash, blocking CI/CD.
mitigation: Validate input sizes, implement timeouts, and retry logic.

threat_description: Script runs with elevated privileges and overwrites critical local files.
mitigation: Run scripts under a non‐privileged user, sandbox file operations, and enforce directory restrictions.

threat_description: An attacker spoofs the replacement script to embed malicious client IDs or scopes.
mitigation: Use authenticated retrieval of scripts, verify signatures, and restrict script sources.

threat_description: Tampering with client ID insertion corrupts the runbook, causing it to target wrong resources.
mitigation: Validate runbook syntax and parameter ranges before upload.

threat_description: Runbook patches are not logged, enabling repudiation of injected client IDs.
mitigation: Audit all patch operations, store diffs in version control.

threat_description: Embedded client IDs in local files are exposed in console output or logs.
mitigation: Mask sensitive parameters in logs and use secure variable stores.

threat_description: Incorrect or malicious client ID injection halts runbook provisioning, causing delays.
mitigation: Implement validation checks, fallback defaults, and alert on failures.

threat_description: The replacement script runs with elevated privileges, modifying restricted runbook resources.
mitigation: Execute under a least-privilege user and enforce file ACLs.

threat_description: An attacker spoofs the VM IP patch script to inject rogue SSH host entries.
mitigation: Validate IP sources, sign scripts, and restrict script modifications.

threat_description: Tampering with the SSH config file introduces incorrect or malicious host mappings.
mitigation: Enforce config file integrity with checksums and restrict write permissions.

threat_description: VM IP patch actions are not logged, allowing undetected changes.
mitigation: Log all config edits with user context in an immutable audit log.

threat_description: Patched SSH configs may leak VM IP or user details in logs.
mitigation: Mask or encrypt sensitive fields in output and logs.

threat_description: Malformed patch causes provisioning failure, blocking VM access.
mitigation: Validate patch format, implement rollbacks, and set execution timeouts.

threat_description: Script runs with root privileges, allowing arbitrary file system modifications.
mitigation: Run under a restricted user, restrict filesystem ACLs, and audit script actions.

threat_description: An attacker manipulates the random seed to produce predictable or colliding IDs.
mitigation: Use cryptographically secure RNG, manage seed entropy, and validate outputs.

threat_description: Predictable IDs allow an adversary to enumerate or guess resource names.
mitigation: Use GUIDs or secure random suffixes and avoid leaking naming patterns.

threat_description: Random generation failure halts resource naming, blocking Terraform apply.
mitigation: Implement fallback ID logic, retry on failure, and monitor errors.

threat_description: ID generation errors are not logged, enabling repudiation of naming issues.
mitigation: Audit RNG successes/failures with timestamps and error details.

threat_description: IDs derived from sensitive data leak context in naming conventions.
mitigation: Ensure randomness is decoupled from sensitive inputs and rotate if needed.

threat_description: Privileged RNG operations exploited to escalate privileges on the host.
mitigation: Run RNG in user context, sandbox the provider plugin, and audit privileges.

threat_description: An attacker spoofs NSG association requests to bind the NIC to a malicious NSG.
mitigation: Enforce Azure AD authentication and validate resource IDs before binding.

threat_description: Tampering with the association changes firewall rules, exposing VM to unauthorized traffic.
mitigation: Use Azure Policy to enforce approved NSGs and lock resource bindings.

threat_description: Association changes are not logged, allowing repudiation of security group modifications.
mitigation: Enable Activity Logs for NIC and NSG assignments, forward to SIEM.

threat_description: Logs of NSG assignments leak internal network structure and security posture.
mitigation: Restrict log access, mask sensitive fields, and encrypt log storage.

threat_description: Removal of NSG association causes network outage for VM.
mitigation: Implement change approval workflows, alerts on NSG disassociation.

threat_description: Malicious NSG associations elevate VM’s network privileges to reach sensitive endpoints.
mitigation: Apply least-privilege NSGs, use Just-In-Time network access, and audit association changes.

threat_description: Adversary tampers with Cosmos DB account keys or configuration to gain unauthorized access or disrupt service.
mitigation: Enable Azure Key Vault integration for keys, restrict key rotation and editing via RBAC, and enable resource locks.

threat_description: Unlogged modifications to the Cosmos DB account (e.g., consistency policy or geo-replication) enable repudiation.
mitigation: Enable Azure Activity Log for all account changes and forward to an immutable audit store.

threat_description: Primary endpoint and key are exposed through misconfigured firewall or network rules.
mitigation: Restrict network access to specific IPs or VNETs, enable private endpoints, and rotate keys regularly.

threat_description: Flooding the database with heavy queries or large writes exhausts throughput, causing degradation.
mitigation: Configure appropriate RU/s limits, enable autoscale, monitor RU consumption, and apply rate limiting at API gateway.

threat_description: An attacker tampers with storage account configuration (e.g., enabling public access or changing CORS) to expose data.
mitigation: Enforce Azure Policy to disallow public blob access, lock CORS settings, and require MFA for config changes.

threat_description: Changes to storage account access policies or network rules are not logged, enabling repudiation.
mitigation: Enable Azure Storage Analytics logging, send logs to secure, tamper-evident storage.

threat_description: Sensitive blobs or configuration metadata are disclosed due to overly permissive CORS or public container settings.
mitigation: Restrict CORS origins to known domains, disable public container access, and use SAS with least privileges.

threat_description: Attackers generate high-volume requests or large object uploads to exhaust storage throughput or capacity.
mitigation: Enable Azure DDoS Protection, configure request rate limiting, and monitor storage metrics for anomalies.

threat_description: An attacker tampers with container ACLs to allow unauthorized blob access.
mitigation: Enforce container-level immutability policies and RBAC for ACL changes.

threat_description: Modifications to container settings are not audited, enabling repudiation.
mitigation: Enable Storage Analytics logs and send to immutable log store.

threat_description: Public container settings leak artifact names or code packages.
mitigation: Disable public access, use private endpoints, and limit SAS scopes.

threat_description: High-volume list or blob operations exhaust storage throughput.
mitigation: Enable request rate limits, monitor metrics, and use DDoS Protection.

threat_description: Prod container ACLs tampered to expose production assets.
mitigation: Apply Azure Policy to enforce private access on prod containers.

threat_description: Changes to prod container not logged, enabling denial of changes.
mitigation: Audit container changes via Activity Logs to immutable storage.

threat_description: Public access on prod container leaks static assets.
mitigation: Restrict CORS and public access to approved origins only.

threat_description: Excessive asset requests degrade storage performance.
mitigation: Use CDN with caching and rate limiting for static assets.

threat_description: Dev container ACLs tampered to expose dev artifacts.
mitigation: Lock dev container ACLs via resource locks and RBAC.

threat_description: Lack of audit logs for dev container changes enables repudiation.
mitigation: Forward storage logs to SIEM with immutability.

threat_description: Dev container public access discloses internal dev assets.
mitigation: Disable public dev container and restrict CORS.

threat_description: Automated scans of dev container degrade performance.
mitigation: Implement request throttling and monitor quotas.

threat_description: VM container ACLs tampered to allow unauthorized script downloads.
mitigation: Enforce private container with VNet Service Endpoint.

threat_description: No audit trail for VM container changes enables repudiation.
mitigation: Enable diagnostics logging and immutable storage for logs.

threat_description: Public VM container leaks provisioning scripts.
mitigation: Restrict blob access to managed identity only.

threat_description: High-frequency VM script requests exhaust container throughput.
mitigation: Implement VNet throttling and monitor metrics.

threat_description: An attacker tampers with function package block blob to inject malicious code.
mitigation: Use signed packages and verify checksums at deploy time.

threat_description: Blob metadata changes are not logged, enabling repudiation of package swaps.
mitigation: Enable blob audit logs and forward to immutable archive.

threat_description: Package blob is publicly readable, leaking proprietary code.
mitigation: Disable public access and use private SAS with least privileges.

threat_description: Large or malformed blob uploads exhaust storage account IOPS.
mitigation: Enforce size limits on uploads and monitor blob metrics.

threat_description: Front-end blob package is tampered to include malicious scripts.
mitigation: Validate front-end package signatures and hashes.

threat_description: Lack of audit for front-end blob changes enables denial of malicious updates.
mitigation: Enable front-end blob change logs in a write-once store.

threat_description: Public front-end blob leaks UI source code.
mitigation: Restrict access via SAS, disable anonymous access.

threat_description: Flood of front-end blob downloads slows storage performance.
mitigation: Use CDN and rate limits for blob endpoints.

threat_description: Static assets blob tampered to serve malicious scripts or defaced content.
mitigation: Enable blob immutability policies and validate asset checksums during deployment.

threat_description: Changes to production static assets are not audited, allowing untracked defacement.
mitigation: Enable storage analytics logs for blob changes and forward to immutable log storage.

threat_description: Publicly accessible static assets leak proprietary UI code or sensitive file paths.
mitigation: Restrict container access via private endpoints or scoped SAS tokens, disable anonymous read.

threat_description: High-volume requests for static assets exhaust storage throughput and increase latency.
mitigation: Use CDN with caching and rate limiting, enable Azure DDoS Protection.

threat_description: Dev assets blob tampered to host malicious test pages or internal endpoints.
mitigation: Apply blob immutability and validate dev artifact integrity in CI.

threat_description: Lack of audit for dev blob updates enables untracked unauthorized changes.
mitigation: Enable storage logging for dev assets and forward to secure SIEM.

threat_description: Dev blob publicly accessible, disclosing test or staging data.
mitigation: Disable anonymous access on dev containers, restrict via SAS with minimal scope.

threat_description: Automated scans against dev blob degrade storage performance.
mitigation: Implement request throttling and monitor blob metrics for abnormal spikes.

threat_description: VM script blob tampered to include malicious initialization scripts.
mitigation: Enable blob signing and verify scripts before VM execution.

threat_description: Absence of audit logs for VM script blob changes enables repudiation.
mitigation: Enable storage analytics logs and forward to immutable logging storage.

threat_description: Publicly exposed VM scripts leak sensitive provisioning details.
mitigation: Restrict container access to managed identities only, disable anonymous access.

threat_description: Frequent VM script fetches overwhelm blob performance, slowing provisioning.
mitigation: Use VNet Service Endpoints and monitor request patterns to throttle as needed.

threat_description: SSH config blob tampered, injecting rogue host entries or credentials.
mitigation: Use signed blobs and validate config integrity on download.

threat_description: No audit trail of config blob updates enables repudiation of malicious patches.
mitigation: Enable blob change logs and store in immutable repository.

threat_description: Config blob publicly accessible leaks VM connection details.
mitigation: Restrict container access to trust boundary and use scoped SAS.

threat_description: Malformed or oversized config blob causes deployment scripts to fail.
mitigation: Validate blob schema and size before applying configuration.

threat_description: SAS token tampered to extend expiry or widen permissions.
mitigation: Generate SAS via Key Vault, validate token signature, and limit lifetime by policy.

threat_description: Unlogged SAS generation allows repudiation of unauthorized token creation.
mitigation: Log all SAS requests and issuance events to immutable audit logs.

threat_description: Leaked SAS token grants unauthorized read/write access to blobs.
mitigation: Use IP-restricted SAS, rotate keys frequently, and monitor usage anomalies.

threat_description: Overuse of SAS token floods storage account, degrading service.
mitigation: Limit SAS scope and lifetime, monitor token usage, and revoke on anomalies.

threat_description: An attacker tampers with NSG rules to open unauthorized ports (e.g., SSH from any).
mitigation: Lock NSG via resource lock, enforce approved rule sets through Azure Policy.

threat_description: NSG rule changes are not logged, enabling repudiation of unauthorized modifications.
mitigation: Enable Azure Activity Logs for NSG changes and forward logs to immutable storage.

threat_description: NSG configuration leaks internal network structure and rule sets to unauthorized viewers.
mitigation: Restrict RBAC on NSG viewing, use just-in-time access, and audit reader roles.

threat_description: Removal or misconfiguration of NSG rules allows DDoS or network floods to VM.
mitigation: Implement DDoS Protection, monitor NSG changes, and set alerts on rule deletions.

threat_description: An attacker tampers with VNet address space causing overlap and enabling malicious routing.
mitigation: Lock VNet changes, enforce IP address policies, and require peer review for updates.

threat_description: VNet modifications are unlogged, preventing attribution of network changes.
mitigation: Enable Azure Activity Logs for VNet configuration and archive logs immutably.

threat_description: Exposure of VNet topology leaks network segmentation details to unauthorized parties.
mitigation: Restrict RBAC read permissions on VNets and use network graph masking in portal.

threat_description: Deletion or misconfiguration of VNet disrupts all attached resources and connectivity.
mitigation: Apply resource locks on VNets, enforce change management, and set alerts on critical changes.

threat_description: An attacker tampers with subnet delegation or NSG associations, exposing sensitive hosts.
mitigation: Enforce Azure Policy for approved subnet configurations and require RBAC control.

threat_description: Subnet changes are not recorded, enabling repudiation of network modifications.
mitigation: Enable diagnostic logging on subnets and forward to immutable SIEM.

threat_description: Leakage of subnet address ranges reveals internal network layout.
mitigation: Limit reader roles, hide network details in portal, and use private link endpoints.

threat_description: Accidental or malicious subnet removal isolates VMs, causing loss of service.
mitigation: Apply resource locks on subnets, enforce peer review, and alert on subnet deletions.

threat_description: An attacker tampers with public IP assignment, redirecting traffic to attacker-controlled endpoint.
mitigation: Lock Public IP resources, enforce Azure Policy to restrict IP reassignments.

threat_description: Public IP changes are not audited, allowing repudiation of malicious IP swaps.
mitigation: Enable Activity Logs for public IP configurations and archive logs securely.

threat_description: Public IP leak exposes VM endpoints to unauthorized scanning or attack.
mitigation: Use Azure Firewall, restrict NSG, and rotate DNS labels periodically.

threat_description: Removal of public IP causes loss of external connectivity to the VM.
mitigation: Apply resource locks, monitor IP association status, and set alerts on changes.

threat_description: An attacker tampers with NIC settings to reroute or intercept VM traffic.
mitigation: Enforce Azure Policy on NIC configurations and lock resource changes.

threat_description: NIC configuration changes are unlogged, enabling repudiation of unauthorized modifications.
mitigation: Enable Activity Logs for NIC assignments and forward to immutable logs.

threat_description: Exposure of NIC properties reveals internal and external IP addresses.
mitigation: Restrict read access, use just-in-time network access, and mask IPs in logs.

threat_description: Detachment or disabling of NIC causes VM network outage.
mitigation: Apply resource locks on NIC, monitor attachment status, and set alerts on changes.

threat_description: An attacker tampers with user-assigned identity to escalate privileges in Automation.
mitigation: Lock identity assignment, enforce Azure Policy for identity usage.

threat_description: Changes to identity assignment are not audited, enabling repudiation of privilege escalations.
mitigation: Enable Azure Activity Logs for identity assignments and store logs immutably.

threat_description: Identity details are disclosed to unauthorized users, exposing principal IDs and roles.
mitigation: Restrict reader roles, use PIM for identity exposure, and audit access.

threat_description: Deletion of identity breaks Automation runbooks, causing loss of functionality.
mitigation: Apply resource locks, monitor identity health, and set alerts on deletions.

threat_description: Local runbook file is tampered, injecting malicious commands into the runbook.
mitigation: Use version control, verify file integrity via checksums, and restrict filesystem ACLs.

threat_description: Modifications to local runbook file are unlogged, enabling repudiation of changes.
mitigation: Audit local file changes, use immutable logs, and enforce code reviews.

threat_description: Runbook contents are exposed on local console or logs, revealing sensitive parameters.
mitigation: Mask sensitive data in logs and use secure secret stores for parameters.

threat_description: Missing or corrupted runbook file causes Automation provisioning failures.
mitigation: Validate file presence and integrity before execution, implement fallback logic.

threat_description: An attacker modifies Terraform CLI invocations in transit, injecting malicious flags.
mitigation: Use secure channels (VPN, TLS), validate CLI arguments in CI scripts.

threat_description: Terraform plan outputs or environment variables leak through CI logs.
mitigation: Redact secrets in logs, restrict CI log access, and use ephemeral workspaces.

threat_description: Network disruption prevents Terraform from receiving developer commands, halting deployment.
mitigation: Use redundant network paths, monitor CI connectivity, and retry on failure.

threat_description: Adversary intercepts API calls to modify resource parameters or inject malicious resources.
mitigation: Enforce TLS with certificate pinning, validate payload signatures.

threat_description: Terraform state or plan outputs leaked during API calls reveal environment details.
mitigation: Encrypt state at rest and in transit, restrict API logging of sensitive data.

threat_description: Throttling or blocking of AzureRM API calls prevents resource provisioning.
mitigation: Implement retry logic, use backoff strategies, and monitor API quotas.

threat_description: In-transit tampering of data insertion commands corrupts database contents.
mitigation: Use encrypted connections (TLS), validate data integrity before DB writes.

threat_description: Endpoint or key leaked over network exposes primary database access.
mitigation: Use managed identity for DB access instead of keys, rotate keys regularly.

threat_description: Excessive write requests from script overwhelm CosmosDB throughput.
mitigation: Implement client-side rate limiting, monitor RU consumption, and use bulk import optimizations.

threat_description: Tampering with patched configuration introduces malicious connection strings.
mitigation: Validate environment templates via schema and signatures before applying.

threat_description: Leaked patched settings expose storage and database credentials.
mitigation: Mask sensitive values in code repo, use Key Vault references in App Settings.

threat_description: Malformed patch operation halts function deployment, causing downtime.
mitigation: Implement validation tests for config files, add rollback on failure.

threat_description: Archive file upload is tampered to include malicious binaries.
mitigation: Sign zip packages, verify signatures after upload.

threat_description: Archive contents leaked during upload expose source code.
mitigation: Use secure channel for uploads, restrict blob write permissions.

threat_description: Large or repeated uploads exhaust storage account throughput.
mitigation: Enforce size limits, implement upload quotas, and monitor metrics.

threat_description: Blob package tampered in transit, injecting malicious code at runtime.
mitigation: Use HTTPS with strict TLS, enable blob encryption in transit and at rest.

threat_description: Code package download is monitored, leaking package contents.
mitigation: Restrict blob read access with SAS tokens tied to function identity.

threat_description: Interruption of blob download prevents function startup, causing outages.
mitigation: Enable retry logic, use local caching, and monitor storage availability.

threat_description: In-transit query tampering corrupts database records.
mitigation: Use encrypted connections and parameterized queries; validate input schemas.

threat_description: Database responses leaked on network expose internal data.
mitigation: Encrypt data in transit, restrict network whitelisting to function VNet.

threat_description: Flood of API requests generates heavy DB load, degrading performance.
mitigation: Implement API rate limiting, use caching for frequent reads, and alert on RU spikes.

threat_description: Blob operations tampered in transit modify or delete user data.
mitigation: Use HTTPS and SAS scoped to least permissions; validate operations.

threat_description: Storage access keys leaked over network expose all blobs.
mitigation: Use managed identities, avoid embedding connection strings, rotate keys regularly.

threat_description: High volume of blob reads/writes saturates storage throughput.
mitigation: Implement client-side throttling, use CDNs for static assets.

threat_description: Uploaded patch operations are tampered to inject malicious asset URLs.
mitigation: Verify patch scripts and manifests via signatures before upload.

threat_description: Patch upload outputs leaked in logs reveal internal container URLs.
mitigation: Mask URLs in logs, use secure environment variables for endpoint references.

threat_description: Patch script errors flood storage account, causing slowdowns.
mitigation: Add retry/exponential backoff in scripts and monitor storage metrics.

threat_description: Asset delivery is tampered to return malicious scripts to clients.
mitigation: Enable CDN with origin integrity checks and use HTTPS-only endpoints.

threat_description: Clients can intercept asset traffic and extract sensitive URLs or keys.
mitigation: Enforce TLS, disable CORS "*", and use signed URLs with short TTL.

threat_description: High-frequency client downloads overload storage endpoints.
mitigation: Use CDN caching and rate limiting; enable DDoS Protection.

threat_description: Request payloads are tampered in transit, injecting malicious UI content.
mitigation: Use HTTPS, validate request payloads on server, and sanitize inputs.

threat_description: Client metadata or tokens leaked in requests expose session data.
mitigation: Use secure cookies, enforce same-site policies, and redact sensitive headers.

threat_description: Flood of HTTP requests to front-end causes service throttle or crash.
mitigation: Implement WAF, rate limiting, and autoscaling for function app front.

threat_description: API requests are tampered to bypass authentication or inject SQL/NoSQL commands.
mitigation: Validate and sanitize inputs, enforce token validation, and use parameterized queries.

threat_description: API request parameters leaked expose application logic or sensitive data paths.
mitigation: Avoid verbose error messages, use generic error codes, and log only non-sensitive data.

threat_description: High volume of API calls overloads function app, causing timeouts.
mitigation: Set request quotas, enable rate limiting in API Management, and use circuit breakers.

threat_description: Front-end asset fetches are tampered to load malicious blobs.
mitigation: Enforce HTTPS, validate asset integrity with SRI, and use signed URLs.

threat_description: Credentials or SAS tokens in fetch requests are exposed to network sniffers.
mitigation: Use managed identity for blob access and remove secrets from client code.

threat_description: Excessive blob fetch requests from front-end degrade storage performance.
mitigation: Use caching at CDN, implement client-side throttling, and monitor request rates.

threat_description: Extension payload is tampered in transit, leading to execution of malicious scripts on VM.
mitigation: Use signed extension packages, enforce TLS, and validate checksums before execution.

threat_description: Extension logs leak sensitive configuration or credentials to VM console.
mitigation: Redact secrets in extensions logs and send to secure log aggregator.

threat_description: Malicious or malformed extension payload crashes VM provisioning.
mitigation: Implement validation and timeouts for VM extension runs.

threat_description: VM retrieval requests are tampered to fetch malicious scripts from storage.
mitigation: Use managed identity for blob access and enforce HTTPS with TLS.

threat_description: Script fetch responses leak storage URLs or credentials to VM logs.
mitigation: Disable verbose logging, rotate SAS tokens, and use private endpoints.

threat_description: High-frequency script fetches slow storage endpoints, delaying VM setup.
mitigation: Cache scripts locally and monitor storage request metrics for anomalies.

threat_description: Runbook execution calls are tampered to run unauthorized operations via ARM API.
mitigation: Use managed identity auth, enforce RBAC, and validate operation scopes.

threat_description: Runbook output leaks resource IDs or credential details in logs.
mitigation: Mask sensitive output, send logs to secure aggregator, and limit log retention.

threat_description: Flood of runbook jobs saturates ARM API, delaying other management operations.
mitigation: Implement job concurrency limits, monitor API call rates, and alert on spikes.

threat_description: Patched runbook upload is tampered to use attacker-controlled client IDs.
mitigation: Validate runbook parameter templates via schema and signatures.

threat_description: Runbook patching outputs leak client IDs and subscription context.
mitigation: Mask sensitive parameters in logs and use secure variable groups.

threat_description: Malformed client ID patch halts runbook creation, blocking automation.
mitigation: Implement validation checks, include rollback on error, and alert on failures.
